title: Tabernaemontana elastica
intro: Tabernaemontana elastica, also known as the bael or Malabar chestnut, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana elastica include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, including the notable compound tabernaemontanine, which is believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tabernaemontana elastica is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana elastica is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Tabernaemontana is a group of tropical and subtropical trees and shrubs native to Asia and the Pacific Islands. The elastica species is a part of this genus, known for its unique properties and uses. The classification of Tabernaemontana elastica is further supported by its distinctive characteristics and morphological features.
uses: Tabernaemontana elastica is known to possess various medicinal properties, with traditional uses in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are often used in herbal remedies to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In some cultures, Tabernaemontana elastica is used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these medicinal uses and to standardize the extraction and preparation of Tabernaemontana elastica for therapeutic purposes.
benefits: Tabernaemontana elastica, also known as the Bungee Tree or Rubber Tree, is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The roots and leaves of the plant are used in herbal remedies to aid in the healing process and to reduce stress. properties: Tabernaemontana elastica is a plant species that has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and pain. constituents: Tabernaemontana elastica contains a range of active constituents, including alkaloids and iridoid glycosides. These compounds have been found to exhibit various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The alkaloids present in the plant, such as tabernaemontanine and voacamine, have been studied for their potential therapeutic effects. Additionally, the iridoid glycosides, like voacangine and voacristine, have been reported to possess antimicrobial and cytotoxic activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological potential of these active constituents.

preparations: Tabernaemontana elastica, also known as the rubber tree or Indian rubber plant, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves are often used to make herbal preparations, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. side_effects: Tabernaemontana elastica, also known as the rubber tree or rubber vine, contains toxic compounds that can cause side effects if ingested or handled improperly. Ingesting the plant's leaves or seeds can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and dermatitis. In rare cases, ingesting large amounts of the plant's toxic compounds can lead to more severe side effects, including seizures and respiratory failure. It is essential to handle the plant with caution and avoid ingesting any part of it.
